_comment: Lost-in-the-middle eval (evals/README.md §3, problem #1 assembly). A shared pool of irrelevant filler facts plus cases, each with the one fact (needle) that answers its question. The eval inserts the needle at the EDGES (first/last slot) vs the MIDDLE of the context block and asks a real model the question; if edge accuracy beats middle accuracy, the model under-attends to the center — validating the primacy/recency placement that pack_working_set implements. Answers are distinctive substrings absent from the fillers.
fillers: The cafeteria serves lunch from 11:30 to 1:30 on weekdays.
All-hands meetings happen on the last Friday of each month.
The office guest wifi network is named Skyline-Guest.
Parking badges are renewed at the start of every calendar year.
The design team collaborates in a shared Figma workspace.
Quarterly engagement surveys are sent out by the People team.
The build pipeline runs on a fleet of Linux runners.
Internal documentation lives in the company wiki.
New hires finish onboarding during their first week.
The shared support inbox is checked roughly every two hours.
Company swag is ordered through the operations portal.
The weekly engineering newsletter goes out on Wednesdays.

cases.question: What port does the Aurora gateway listen on?
cases.needle: The Aurora gateway listens for traffic on port 8443.

cases.question: Who approved the Q3 budget?
cases.needle: The Q3 budget was formally approved by Dana Reyes.

cases.question: What is the command to roll back the Ledger service?
cases.needle: Roll back the Ledger service with the command ledgerctl revert --to last-stable.

cases.question: Which city hosts the primary datacenter?
cases.needle: The primary datacenter is hosted in Reykjavik.

cases.question: How many times does the ingest worker retry?
cases.needle: The ingest worker retries at most seven times before giving up.

cases.question: Which team owns the Sentinel canary system?
cases.needle: The Sentinel canary system is owned by the Release Engineering team.
